headerDisplay: Homebridge plugin for the Aqara Presence Sensor FP2. Exposes the main occupancy, light level, and per-zone occupancy services via HAP-over-WiFi. Requires the FP2 reset (10s long-press on its button) to obtain a fresh setup pin.
footerDisplay: For setup, troubleshooting, and zone configuration see [the README](https://github.com/hermandeknop/homebridge-fp2#readme).

name.title: Display Name
name.description: Shown in the Home app for the main accessory.

host.title: FP2 identifier
host.description:
How the plugin finds your FP2. Accepts:
• mDNS bonjour name like `Presence-Sensor-FP2-A73D` (recommended — stable across DHCP and factory resets)
• mDNS hostname like `Presence-Sensor-FP2-A73D.local`
• IPv4 address like `192.168.1.123` (only stable if you've reserved the DHCP lease)

Once the plugin has paired with the FP2, it also tracks the HAP device id and uses that to follow the FP2 across IP changes — but on first run, this field is the only way to find it.

port.title: HAP Port
port.description: HAP-over-IP port. The FP2 advertises this via mDNS; usually it does not need to change.

pin.title: Setup Pin
pin.description: 8-digit HAP setup pin formatted as ###-##-###. Reset the FP2 to obtain a fresh pin if it is already paired with an Apple Home.

exposeZones.title: Expose individual zones
exposeZones.description: Create a separate Occupancy Sensor for each zone configured in the Aqara app.

pollIntervalSeconds.title: Poll interval (seconds)
pollIntervalSeconds.description: Fallback polling. Real-time updates use HAP event subscriptions; polling only runs as a safety net.

excludedZones.title: Excluded zone names
excludedZones.description: Zone names (as configured in the Aqara app) to skip when creating per-zone Occupancy sensors.
